question_text: A microwave oven uses microwaves to heat food. Explain why microwaves are suitable for this purpose, and describe what happens to the energy of the microwaves.
correct_answer: Microwaves are suitable because they have the right amount of energy to make water and other molecules in food vibrate/rotate. As the molecules vibrate, their kinetic energy increases, which heats up the food. The electromagnetic energy of the waves is converted into thermal (heat) energy.
explanation: This tests understanding of how electromagnetic waves interact with matter. Microwaves are specifically absorbed by polar molecules like water, causing them to vibrate and generate heat—this is a real application that shows energy conversion in action.
